methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed stage climate & energy company that has raised $6M to date. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers operating in climate and energy, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $6M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for pre-seed to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $15M. The peer-median method is not applicable; while 21 same-sector peers exist in climate & energy, filtering by stage, timeline, and valuation tier produced fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer set unreliable. An all-sector median would introduce undue noise given the specialized nature of frontier energy technology.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M (low) to $17M (high), with a median point of $15M. This range reflects a single methodological anchor, which is a material limitation. Key uncertainties include technology validation milestones, capital efficiency relative to comparable-stage peers in capital-intensive sectors, and founder execution track record in scaling hardware ventures. The narrow applicability of peer comparables underscores reliance on stage-typical progression assumptions rather than market-tested company comparables.
